steps.title: Find the mixed reaction
steps.content: The student's claim is not just that cultured meat may help the environment. It is that people have mixed feelings about cultured meat, and those mixed feelings may make worldwide adoption harder.

steps.title: Separate attitudes from facts
steps.content: Choice A discusses consumer views about using less packaging for meat products, so it is about packaging rather than cultured meat. Choice C explains how cells are grown in lab-grown meat, which is a production detail rather than evidence about public attitudes. Choice D gives advocates' environmental argument, but it is one-sided support. Choice B shows the needed tension: consumers saw a possible environmental benefit but were still unwilling to try eating cultured meat themselves. The answer is B.
